title: The Bi Sexual

jokes.text: You don't have to worry about choking because I'm great at the heimlich.
jokes.setup: Woman offers her number and reassurance about dining safety
jokes.punchline: Aggressive promotion of heimlich skills as a dating asset
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection - heimlich as pickup line becomes plot relevant later. Well-structured with good economy.

jokes.text: Oh, well, God, I mean if I wasn't gonna give you my number before, that just did. That's an aggressive skill.
jokes.setup: Woman responds to heimlich offer
jokes.explanation: Unexpected characterization of a safety skill as 'aggressive' - good wordplay with excellent economy.

jokes.text: It'd be great if you could get out of here.
jokes.setup: Romantic meeting going well
jokes.explanation: Perfect Curb-style social awkwardness delivered deadpan. Economy and character work excellent.

jokes.text: You know what a lesbian brings on a second date? A u-haul.
jokes.setup: Discussing lesbian dating patterns
jokes.punchline: U-haul stereotype about moving in quickly
jokes.explanation: Classic lesbian stereotype joke. Well-delivered but not particularly original. Good character integration for Rosie.

jokes.text: I looked at her and I thought hyphen name.
jokes.setup: Rosie thinking about future with new woman
jokes.explanation: Brilliant compressed storytelling - entire lesbian relationship timeline in three words. Excellent economy and character specificity.

jokes.text: Smiling at an old bald man.
jokes.setup: Larry describing woman's interest in him
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry self-deprecation. Perfect character voice but predictable territory.

jokes.text: Yeah, I knew from her name. O'Donnell, Irish, duh.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining how he knew Jane was Jewish
jokes.punchline: Incorrectly identifying O'Donnell as Irish name indicating Jewishness
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - Larry's confidence in completely wrong logic. Good setup for confusion reveal.

jokes.text: Obviously she's bi-sexual.
jokes.setup: Discovering they both got Jane's number
jokes.explanation: Larry's logical leap delivered with characteristic certainty. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: What is that anyway? I mean, pick a side already, right?
jokes.setup: Discussing bisexuality
jokes.punchline: Reducing complex sexuality to team selection
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry oversimplification. Perfectly in character though territory is somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: They have to have sex with everyone? They have to do everything? Half the population isn't enough for them?
jokes.setup: Larry's bisexuality complaints
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with Larry's characteristic mathematical outrage. Perfect structure and character voice.

jokes.text: Look, in baseball the tie goes to the runner. In situations like this, tie goes to the hetero.
jokes.setup: Larry establishing dating rules
jokes.punchline: Application of baseball rules to sexuality competition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parallel structure creating new social rule. Excellent economy and perfect Larry logic. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: I came alive in areas that have been dry like the Sahara desert.
jokes.setup: Rosie describing her physical response to Jane
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with vivid metaphor. Character-appropriate oversharing.

jokes.text: She hit my base first cleanly before she even saw you.
jokes.setup: Continuing baseball metaphor from Larry
jokes.explanation: Good callback extending Larry's baseball metaphor into sexual territory. Solid structure.

jokes.text: She's a dyke. Deal with it.
jokes.setup: Larry's final argument in the competition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry bluntness with maximum economy. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Ho ho! Look who's in the motherfucking house! Ha ha! Leon in the motherfucking house!
jokes.setup: Leon's dramatic entrance
jokes.explanation: Pure Leon character comedy. Excessive profanity perfectly captures his energy and personality.

jokes.text: Let me tell you something. This motherfucking car has got some heart, man. I was surprised. I thought it was a bitch-ass car.
jokes.setup: Leon reviewing Larry's car performance
jokes.explanation: Leon's unique voice anthropomorphizing the car. 'Bitch-ass car' is perfectly Leon. Great character work.

jokes.text: You got to have a photo I.D. for that, you know what I'm saying? Fuck that.
jokes.setup: Larry asking why Leon drove instead of flying
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of avoiding ID requirements
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Leon's relationship with authority. Perfectly in character with good economy.

jokes.text: I got eight motherfuckers up in this car.
jokes.setup: Larry asking about car capacity
jokes.explanation: Leon's casual exaggeration about impossible car capacity. Good absurdist humor with excellent economy.

jokes.text: They some tricky motherfuckers. Frankly, I don't know what I'm doing down there. Ladies know that shit.
jokes.setup: Discussing lesbian sexual advantages
jokes.punchline: Leon's honest admission of sexual incompetence
jokes.explanation: Unexpected vulnerability from Leon mixed with his characteristic profanity. Good honesty mixed with humor.

jokes.text: They know every nook and cranny. You know, every nook and cranny.
jokes.setup: Larry agreeing about lesbian advantages
jokes.explanation: Weak repetitive humor. Larry just echoing Leon's better observation without adding value.

jokes.text: First of all, I would fucking throw the fuck up. I would regurgitate my own shit.
jokes.setup: Leon describing hypothetical attraction to Larry
jokes.explanation: Leon's escalating disgust becomes absurd biology. Perfect character voice with good escalation structure.

jokes.text: You think I'd go out with a guy wearing a green wife-beater?
jokes.setup: Larry responding to Leon's rejection
jokes.explanation: Larry's petty fashion criticism as defensive response. Good character work with specific detail.

jokes.text: Well, we really don't see each other in L.A. So why-- If we're in one city all the time and we never have lunch in that city, why would we have lunch in the new city?
jokes.setup: Duckstein asking Larry to lunch in New York
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic applied to social relationships. Excellent structure and completely in character. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: We don't spend time together because I don't want to spend time together.
jokes.setup: Duckstein suggesting they're not friends because they don't spend time together
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry brutal honesty with perfect economy. Cuts through social niceties with surgical precision.

jokes.text: There will be no lunch, no breakfast or dinner. There won't be a snack. There won't be coffee. There won't be a drink.
jokes.setup: Larry rejecting Duckstein's lunch invitation
jokes.punchline: Comprehensive list of rejected food-based socializing
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation covering all possible food encounters. Perfect Larry thoroughness and cruelty.

jokes.text: Life is like this... And what you've done is made it like this. I want to make that smaller.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining life philosophy to Duckstein
jokes.punchline: Hand gestures showing shrinking social circle
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy with Larry literally demonstrating his desire to shrink his social world. Good physical element.

jokes.text: Maybe the cat knew I was coming. Oh. Maybe that's why I was throwing up a minute ago.
jokes.setup: Jane's cat is throwing up, Larry suggests it knew he was coming
jokes.punchline: Larry claiming he was also throwing up
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor with empathetic nausea concept. Well-structured callback to initial observation.

jokes.text: Yeah, ever since I met you I can't stop puking. I don't know what it is.
jokes.setup: Building on the nausea theme
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the nausea theme. Character-appropriate honesty creates nice cringe moment.

jokes.text: You know, you're in the take-out business, you got two things to do: Get the order right, secure the food.
jokes.setup: Spilled soup disaster
jokes.punchline: Reducing restaurant business to two simple rules
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry oversimplification of complex business. Perfect character voice and highly quotable.

jokes.text: I had Sushi at Yankee stadium, which you would not think is good Sushi, but it was actually really good Sushi.
jokes.setup: Jane mentioning where she had sushi
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about unexpected venue quality. Functional but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: What did Rosie think of your apartment? She didn't see my apartment. Really? She wasn't here? No. Oh. Poor thing.
jokes.setup: Larry fishing for information about Jane and Rosie
jokes.punchline: Jane's simple answers deflating Larry's assumptions
jokes.explanation: Good awkward interrogation sequence. Larry's transparent fishing expedition creates nice uncomfortable moment.

jokes.text: Basically, man to man, I think an apology would be nice.
jokes.setup: Japanese restaurant worker offering to remake order
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry prioritizing emotional satisfaction over practical solution. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: I am sorry. Oh, the bow. Oh my gosh. Oh. I can't quibble with the bow.
jokes.setup: Worker apologizing with traditional Japanese bow
jokes.punchline: Larry's delighted reaction to the gesture
jokes.explanation: Good setup for later bow comedy. Larry's genuine appreciation for cultural gesture is endearing.

jokes.text: Who goes out on Monday? Someone who's really desperate to have a date, I suppose, calls somebody for a Monday night.
jokes.setup: Larry learning Rosie had Monday date with Jane
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry creating arbitrary social rules to dismiss competition. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: She was too busy moaning to laugh.
jokes.setup: Larry claiming women usually laugh at his moves
jokes.explanation: Larry's awkward sexual boasting. Good economy but somewhat predictable territory for the character.

jokes.text: She knows all the words to 'Cats,' okay? Even the British version. She enjoyed 'Chess' the musical. Saw it three times. She saw 'Carrie.' It was only open two nights.
jokes.setup: Rosie explaining Jane's Broadway credentials
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from mainstream to legendary Broadway failures. Great insider knowledge and perfect structure.

jokes.text: We are fucking men, Larry. What do we have, man? What the fuck do we have that she doesn't have?
jokes.setup: Leon's motivational speech about male advantages
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Leon's elaborate metaphor. Perfect character voice building anticipation.

jokes.text: What's below your fucking bat? What dangles below your bat? Balls. We got bats and balls, right? Okay, what do ladies have? Gloves. What do gloves do? Catch the goddamn balls.
jokes.setup: Leon's motivational metaphor about male advantages
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extended metaphor perfectly delivered in Leon's voice. Creative structure and highly quotable. Peak Leon philosophy.

jokes.text: Bats and balls run the fucking world. The woman is on this earth to catch balls.
jokes.setup: Continuation of Leon's baseball metaphor
jokes.punchline: Cosmic conclusion about gender roles via sports equipment
jokes.explanation: Peak Leon pseudo-philosophy. Absurd cosmic conclusion delivered with complete conviction. Extremely quotable.

jokes.text: You want me to juice?
jokes.setup: Leon suggesting Larry needs enhancement
jokes.explanation: Good setup for performance enhancement storyline - Larry's naivety about drugs

jokes.text: The penis is an extraordinary instrument, Jane. Sometimes it surprises even me.
jokes.setup: Post-coital conversation
jokes.punchline: Larry's pompous assessment of his own anatomy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry pretentiousness about his sexual performance. Good callback to Leon's metaphor with Larry's characteristic ego.

jokes.text: That is not apology to you. No, he was not sorry at all. He a little dismissive of you.
jokes.setup: Larry asking about quality of previous apology bow
jokes.punchline: Confirmation that Larry received inadequate apology
jokes.explanation: Good callback validating Larry's instincts about cultural respect. Sets up confrontation nicely.

jokes.text: A shit bow? Shit bow. A shit bow I got. I got a shit bow.
jokes.setup: Learning about inadequate bow quality
jokes.explanation: Larry's outrage over cultural slight. Repetition works well for building indignation. 'Shit bow' highly quotable.

jokes.text: I've known like four decades of women that you've dated. None of them ever ever talks about your performance. You're a singles hitter your whole life, now all of a sudden you're Mr. power hitter?
jokes.setup: Rosie questioning Larry's sudden sexual prowess
jokes.punchline: Baseball metaphor for Larry's sexual performance history
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to baseball theme with brutal assessment of Larry's sexual history. Perfect structure and character knowledge.

jokes.text: You're Mr. grand slam? Mr. get anybody, even Jane, huh? You doing something, Lar? What do you do?
jokes.setup: Rosie suspicious of Larry's sudden success
jokes.explanation: Good setup for performance enhancement revelation

jokes.text: I have never taken performance enhancing drugs ever. Period.
jokes.setup: Rosie asking if Larry is juicing
jokes.punchline: Categorical denial delivered with suspicious emphasis
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of athlete steroid denials. Larry's over-emphasis makes it obviously false. Good dramatic irony.

jokes.text: You know how to do the heimlich. You could have, you know. Yeah, I know how to do the heimlich. You do. If you only went to lunch with him.
jokes.setup: Learning Duckstein choked and was hospitalized
jokes.punchline: Connecting Larry's rejection to Duckstein's near-death experience
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback connecting opening scene to consequences. Perfect irony of Larry's social cruelty having real consequences.

jokes.text: It's like being with a great guy who happens to have a vagina and knows the statistics of what Roy White hit in 1971.
jokes.setup: Larry describing Jane's appeal
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific baseball knowledge combined with anatomy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry description of ideal woman. Specific baseball reference makes it perfectly Larry. Excellent character specificity.

jokes.text: Your bow was like 15°. I asked him about it and he said that what you did is actually dismissive.
jokes.setup: Larry returning to restaurant with bow research
jokes.punchline: Specific degree measurement of inadequate bow
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Larry's characteristic over-precision. Measuring cultural gestures in degrees perfectly Larry.

jokes.text: He said shit bow? Yes, those were his words. Shit bow.
jokes.setup: Larry revealing Japanese man's assessment
jokes.punchline: Worker's shock at the harsh judgment
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to bow storyline. Worker's mortification at receiving harsh cultural criticism works well.

jokes.text: Whoa, you dropped something. What is this? Are you juicing?
jokes.setup: Larry's pill falls during bow demonstration
jokes.explanation: Perfect plot convergence - bow demonstration leads to drug discovery. Excellent structure bringing storylines together.

jokes.text: Everybody's doing it! I was just leveling the playing field!
jokes.setup: Larry caught with performance drugs
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of athlete excuses with Larry's characteristic rationalization. Great payoff to baseball theme.

jokes.text: This could be our first meal together. Get my friend some lunch. My new friend some lunch. You know something? This is so ironic. We're having lunch after all.
jokes.setup: Duckstein in hospital bed after choking
jokes.punchline: Forcing hospital meal as the lunch Larry rejected
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark irony bringing the episode full circle. Duckstein's forced gratitude after near-death creates perfect Curb ending.

tmdb_overview: Larry competes with Rosie O’Donnell for the same woman, explores the nuances of Japanese bows, and refuses lunch with an LA acquaintance.
